policy: Corrections are deliberately narrow, source-hash guarded, and documentation-only. A correction may clarify a parsed field or argument contract but may not alter a Rust ABI signature, native symbol, provider closure, or final disposition. Reusable profiles override only the named argument rows and are materialized into the per-routine inventory after the referenced source hash is verified.

F.description: Synchronous integrand callback. It receives one integration abscissa and returns the function value; it must remain valid for the call, must not retain the native pointer, and must not unwind through Fortran.

EPSREL.description: Input requested relative integration accuracy. When EPSABS is nonpositive, the source minimum relative tolerance applies.

KEY.description: Input selector for the local Gauss-Kronrod integration rule.

RESULT.description: Writable output approximation to the integral.

ABSERR.description: Writable output estimate of the absolute integration error; it is intended to bound abs(I-RESULT).

NEVAL.description: Writable output number of integrand evaluations.

IER.description: Writable completion and error indicator. Its documented values are rendered in the status table.

LIMIT.description: Input maximum number of subintervals and minimum required IWORK length. LIMIT must be at least 1.

LENW.description: Input declared WORK length. LENW must be at least 4*LIMIT.

LAST.description: Writable output number of subintervals produced; it determines the significant entries in IWORK and WORK.

IWORK.description: Writable integer workspace of at least LIMIT elements. Its leading entries order subinterval error estimates as specified by the selected source.

WORK.description: Writable real workspace of at least LENW elements. Its four LIMIT-strided segments hold left endpoints, right endpoints, subintegral estimates, and error estimates.

XNU.description: Input fractional starting order. `XNU` must lie strictly between -1 and 1.

X.description: Input evaluation point. `X` must be strictly positive; the returned sequence is evaluated at this point and native code does not modify it.

NIN.description: Input nonzero signed sequence length. `abs(NIN)` is the required result-array length; a positive value advances the order and a negative value steps it downward from `XNU`.

BKE.description: Writable output array with at least `abs(NIN)` elements. On return `BKE[I]` holds `exp(X) * K_(XNU+I)(X)` for the requested order sequence; native code retains no pointer.

XNU.description: Input fractional starting order. `XNU` must lie strictly between -1 and 1.

X.description: Input evaluation point. `X` must be strictly positive; native code does not modify it.

NIN.description: Input nonzero signed sequence length. `abs(NIN)` is the required result-array length; a positive value advances the order and a negative value steps it downward from `XNU`.

BK.description: Writable output array with at least `abs(NIN)` elements. On return `BK[I]` holds the modified Bessel K value at order `XNU+I`; native code retains no pointer.

MP.description: Input M-direction coupling selector. `MP=0` requests the periodic coefficient case and requires the endpoint off-diagonal coefficients to be nonzero; `MP=1` selects the noncyclic endpoint case where those endpoint coefficients are zero.

IERROR.description: Writable status output. `0` means success; `1` means `M < 5`; `2` means `N < 5`; `3` means `IDIMY < M`; `4` reports a coefficient-array failure; and `5` reports an invalid negative product in the tridiagonal coefficient condition. Except for `0`, no solution is produced.

ZIN.description: Input complex argument to the digamma function. The routine evaluates the logarithmic derivative of gamma at this value; poles and values too near a negative integer can trigger the SLATEC error handler.

X.description: Input positive double-precision argument. The function returns the exponentially scaled order-one modified Bessel K value at `X`; native code does not modify the scalar.

MROWS.description: Number of rows in the least-squares block `[E:F]`. With accumulation option `IOPT(1)`, this is a writable returned count of accumulated rows; otherwise it is an input count used with `MDW`, `MCON`, and `NCOLS`.

RNORM.description: Writable output for the Euclidean length of the final residual vector of the constrained least-squares problem.

RW.description: Writable real workspace with at least `6*NCOLS + 5*MCON` elements. It is scratch storage for the bounded constrained solve and native code retains no pointer.

MDW.description: Input leading dimension of `W`. It must satisfy `MDW >= MROWS`; smaller values are input errors.

RNORM.description: Writable output for the minimum residual-vector length. It is meaningful when the returned `MODE` reports a solution.

XDATA.description: Readable array of `NDATA` abscissae for the discrete observations. The selected source permits unsorted input; native code does not retain the pointer.

YDATA.description: Readable array of `NDATA` ordinates paired with `XDATA`. These values are the observed data used in the weighted B-spline fit and are not retained.

COEFF.description: Writable B-spline coefficient array. When `MDEOUT=1`, it receives the `NBKPT-NORD` fitted coefficients; when the fit is incomplete or invalid, the selected source sets its entries to zero.

W.description: Persistent work array whose allocated length is supplied by `LW`. Its contents must not be modified by the caller between `MDEIN=1,2,2,...` calls; its leading `(NBKPT-NORD+3)*(NORD+1)` entries can be passed to `DFC` as an old problem when `MDEOUT` is 1 or 2.

X.description: Input current integration abscissa from `DSTEPS`. It and the history arguments must be from the same unmodified `DSTEPS` state.

Y.description: Readable current solution vector from `DSTEPS`, with at least `NEQN` elements.

NEQN.description: Input number of differential equations. It is the required length of `Y`, `YOUT`, and `YPOUT` and the first dimension of `PHI`.

KOLD.description: Input interpolation order saved by `DSTEPS`. It controls how many columns of the `PHI` history are used and must be passed unchanged from that integrator state.

PHI.description: Readable `DSTEPS` history matrix with Fortran shape `(NEQN, 16)`. It defines the local interpolation polynomial and must not be synthesized independently.

IVC.description: Readable interpolation-cache control index supplied by `DSTEPS`; it selects cached data in `IV` and `OW` when the order changes.

IV.description: Readable integer interpolation cache of length 10 supplied by `DSTEPS`. It is part of the persistent integrator state and must be passed unchanged.

KGI.description: Readable `DSTEPS` interpolation-history order marker used to decide whether cached `GI` values apply.

GI.description: Readable interpolation cache of length 11 supplied by `DSTEPS`. It stores precomputed integral factors and must remain consistent with `KGI` and `KOLD`.

ALPHA.description: Readable `DSTEPS` coefficient array of length 12 used to reconstruct the interpolation factors.

OG.description: Readable `DSTEPS` interpolation-history array of length 13 used when evaluating the local polynomial.

OW.description: Readable `DSTEPS` interpolation-cache array of length 12. It is indexed through `IVC` and `IV` and is not independent workspace.

OX.description: Readable previous integration abscissa from `DSTEPS`; together with `X` it defines the interpolation interval.

OY.description: Readable previous solution vector from `DSTEPS`, with at least `NEQN` elements. It supplies the endpoint data for the smooth interpolant.

W.description: Writable real work array for the rank-revealing QR solve. It requires `5*N` elements when `RE` and `AE` are scalar, `4*N` when either is vector-valued, and `3*N` when both are vector-valued. Its leading entries are persistent factorization state for an `INFO=1` continuation call.

Writable real work array for the rank-revealing LQ solve. It requires `5*M` elements when `RE` and `AE` are scalar, `4*M` when either is vector-valued, and `3*M` when both are vector-valued. Its leading entries are persistent factorization state for an `INFO=1` continuation call.

DY1.description: Readable second component of the input two-vector used to construct the modified Givens transformation. The routine uses it to form the rotation but leaves this scalar unchanged.

SY1.description: Readable second component of the input two-vector used to construct the modified Givens transformation. The routine uses it to form the rotation but leaves this scalar unchanged.

DINV.description: Writable array of at least `N` entries. On return it stores the inverse diagonal factor `D^-1` from the no-fill incomplete LDU preconditioner; it is consumed by the related SLAP solve routines.

N.description: Input order of the sparse linear system and required vector length.

B.description: Readable right-hand-side vector of length `N` for the SLAP lower-triangular preconditioner solve. The computed vector is returned through `X`, not by retaining `B`.

X.description: Writable output vector of length `N`. The interface computes the lower-triangular preconditioner solve `L^-1 * B` into this storage.

NELT.description: Input number of stored nonzero entries in the sparse arrays `IA`, `JA`, and `A`.

IA.description: Readable integer sparse-index array with at least `NELT` entries. Together with `JA` it describes the SLAP column-format matrix or preconditioner data.

JA.description: Readable integer sparse-offset/index array with at least `NELT` entries as declared by this interface. Together with `IA` and `A` it identifies the SLAP column-format entries.

A.description: Readable sparse value array with at least `NELT` entries, stored in the SLAP column format described by `IA` and `JA`.

ISYM.description: Input sparse-storage symmetry flag. `0` means all nonzero entries are present; `1` means symmetric storage contains only the lower triangle.

RWORK.description: Readable real work/state array initialized for the underlying SLAP `*SLI2` routine. Its offsets are supplied through `IWORK`; native code uses it as preconditioner storage and does not retain it.

PQA.description: Writable extended-range mantissa array. Together with `IPQA`, it returns the requested Legendre-function vector; element `I` represents `PQA[I] * radix^IPQA[I]`. Its required length is `MU2-MU1+1` or `NUDIFF+1`, according to the selected vector form.

IPQA.description: Writable extended-range exponent array paired element-for-element with `PQA`. A zero entry means the corresponding value is directly representable in the routine precision; callers must inspect nonzero entries before treating `PQA` alone as the result.

DARG.description: Input real argument. With `MODE=1` it is the Legendre argument and must lie in [-1,1]; with `MODE=2` it is an angle strictly between -pi and pi and the routine uses its cosine.

SARG.description: Input real argument. With `MODE=1` it is the Legendre argument and must lie in [-1,1]; with `MODE=2` it is an angle strictly between -pi and pi and the routine uses its cosine.

DPN.description: Writable extended-range mantissa array. Together with `IPN`, element `I` represents the normalized Legendre value for order `MU1+I-1` in the selected extended-range representation.

SPN.description: Writable extended-range mantissa array. Together with `IPN`, element `I` represents the normalized Legendre value for order `MU1+I-1` in the selected extended-range representation.

ISIG.description: Writable output estimate of decimal digits lost to rounding in the extended-range normalized-Legendre calculation. Subtract it from the significant digits in the input argument to estimate retained result precision away from zeros.

X.description: Input double-precision argument at which the logarithm of the absolute gamma value is evaluated.

DLGAM.description: Writable output for the natural logarithm of the absolute value of the gamma function at `X`.

SGNGAM.description: Writable output sign of the gamma function at `X`; the selected implementation returns either 1 or -1.

TYPE.description: Input logical selector. Set true to compute algebraically smallest eigenvalues and false to compute algebraically largest eigenvalues; it also determines the ordering of `W` and the sentinel stored in `E2(1)`.

FNC.description: Synchronous scalar equation callback. It receives a readable length-`NEQ` current or finite-difference-perturbed solution vector and a one-based equation index `K` in `1..=NEQ`, then returns equation `K`; it must not retain pointers or unwind through Fortran.

NEQ.description: Input number of equations and unknowns. It must be positive and is the required `X` length.

X.description: Mutable length-`NEQ` solution vector. It supplies the initial estimate and is overwritten with the current iterate; the native finite-difference calculation temporarily perturbs components before callback evaluations.

RTOLX.description: Input nonnegative relative solution-increment tolerance used with `ATOLX` in the componentwise convergence test.

ATOLX.description: Input nonnegative absolute solution-increment tolerance. A positive value is useful when a solution component can be zero.

TOLF.description: Input nonnegative residual tolerance. Residual convergence requires every equation residual magnitude to be at most `TOLF`; its value must match equation scaling.

IFLAG.description: Input/output control and status integer. Input `0` uses defaults and input `-1` enables `IW(1..=2)` controls; on return it reports the documented convergence or error status.

RW.description: Mutable real workspace of at least `1 + 6*NEQ + NEQ*(NEQ + 1)/2` elements. `RW(1)` receives the residual norm on return.

LRW.description: Input declared length of `RW`; it must be at least `1 + 6*NEQ + NEQ*(NEQ + 1)/2`.

IW.description: Mutable integer workspace of at least `3 + NEQ` elements. With input `IFLAG=-1`, `IW(1)=-1` requests iteration output and `IW(2)` supplies a positive iteration limit; `IW(3)` receives the iteration count.

LIW.description: Input declared length of `IW`; it must be at least `3 + NEQ`.

records.reason: The selected QUADPACK prologue intentionally interleaves output, error, dimensioning, and workspace paragraphs; the profile preserves their verified semantics without cross-contamination.
records.reviewer_note: M3 semantic review: arguments, IER values, and four WORK segments checked against dqag.f.

records.reason: Precision sibling of DQAG with the same verified call contract; the profile records only semantics common to signatures with matching argument order and shapes.
records.evidence_lines: QAG selected source prologue lines 22-136; DQAG precision-sibling comparison
records.reviewer_note: M3 semantic review: single-precision signature and callback structure match QAG source exactly.

records.reason: The selected source states the sequence contract in one routine-level paragraph rather than argument rows.
records.reviewer_note: M3 semantic review: starting order, signed count, and scaled sequence result checked against dbskes.f.

records.reason: The selected source documents the result at routine scope rather than as individual argument rows.
records.reviewer_note: M3 semantic review: DLGAM receives log(abs(gamma(X))) and SGNGAM follows the selected sign computation.

records.reason: The selected source describes the callback and workspace relationship at routine scope; this source-hash-guarded profile materializes the exact reviewed argument contract.
records.evidence_lines: SOS selected source prologue lines 50-164 and SOSEQS executable callback call sites
records.reviewer_note: SOS callback ABI, workspace formulas, IFLAG values, and finite-difference callback behavior reviewed against sos.f and soseqs.f.

records.reason: The selected source describes the callback and workspace relationship at routine scope; this source-hash-guarded profile materializes the exact reviewed argument contract.
